volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 4.2.575 - Section 4.2.631b)

sections.label: Section 4.2.575
sections.original: boníne an malí sint , id haúd quaeritánt ;
sections.translation: whether they are deserving or undeserving , about that they don ’ t enquire . Their property is more enquired about , than what the reputation of their clients is for honor . If any person is poor and not dishonest , he is considered worthless ; but if a rich man is dishonest , he is considered a good client .

sections.label: Section 4.2.580
sections.original: quí neque legés neque aequóm bonum usquám colunt ,
sections.translation: Those who neither regard laws nor any good or justice at all , the same have zealous patrons . What has been entrusted to them , they deny to have been so entrusted ; men full of litigation , rapacious , and fraudulent ; who have acquired their property either by usury or by perjury ; their whole pleasure is in litigation .

sections.label: Section 4.2.585
sections.original: eís ubi dicitur dies , simul patronis dicitur .
sections.translation: When the day for trial is appointed , at the same time it is mentioned to their patrons , in order that they may plead for them , about what they have done amiss . Before the people [*] , or at law before the Praetor , or before the Aedile , is the cause tried . Just so , this day , a certain dependant has kept me very much engaged , nor was it allowed me to do what I wished , or in company with whom I wished ; so fast did he stick to me , so much did he detain me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.590
sections.original: ápud aediles pro eius factis plurumisque pessumisque
sections.translation: Before the Aedile , in behalf of his doings , very many and very disgraceful , did I plead his cause ; a compromise I obtained , obscure and perplexed — more than enough I said , and than I needed to say , that surety for him [*] might end this litigation . What did he do ? Well , what ? He gave bail . And never did I at any time see any person more clearly detected ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.595
sections.original: omnibus male factis testes tres aderant acerrumi .
sections.translation: three very adverse witnesses against all his misdeeds were there . May all the Gods confound him , he has so spoilt this day for me ; and myself as well , who ever this day beheld the Forum with my eyes . I ordered a breakfast to be prepared ; my mistress is expecting me , I ’ m sure ; as soon as ever I had the opportunity ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.600
sections.original: properavi abire de foro . iratast , credo , nunc mihi ;
sections.translation: I made haste immediately to leave the Forum . Now , I suppose , she ’ s angry with me ; the mantle , however , will appease her that I gave her , the one I took away to-day from my wife and carried to Erotium here .

sections.label: Section 4.2.602
sections.translation: What say you now ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.602b
sections.translation: That I ’ m unfortunately married to a worthless fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.2.602c
sections.translation: Do you perfectly hear what he says ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.603
sections.translation: Quite well .

sections.label: Section 4.2.603b
sections.original: Sí sapiam , hinc intro ábeam , ubi mi bene sít .
sections.translation: If I am wise , I shall be going hence in-doors , where it may be comfortable for me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.603c
sections.translation: Stop ; on the contrary , it shall be uncomfortable .

sections.label: Section 4.2.604
sections.translation: By heavens , you have assuredly taken that away from me at a heavy usury ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.604b
sections.translation: such is the return [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.605
sections.original: Clanculum te istaec flagitia facere censebas potis ?
sections.translation: Did you suppose that you could possibly commit these villanies unknown to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.606
sections.translation: Wife , what ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.606b
sections.translation: Do you ask me that ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.606c
sections.translation: Do you wish me to ask him ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.607
sections.translation: away with your caresses from me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.607b
sections.translation: Do you persist in it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.607c
sections.translation: Why are you offended with me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.608b
sections.translation: You ought to know .

sections.label: Section 4.2.608c
sections.translation: The rascal knows , but he pretends not to know .

sections.label: Section 4.2.609
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.609b
sections.translation: The mantle .

sections.label: Section 4.2.609c
sections.translation: The mantle ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.609d
sections.translation: A certain person has taken a mantle .

sections.label: Section 4.2.609e
sections.translation: Why are you alarmed ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.610
sections.translation: For my part , I ’ m not alarmed at all —

sections.label: Section 4.2.610b
sections.translation: ( aside ) except about one thing ; the mantle makes [*] my face mantle .

sections.label: Section 4.2.611
sections.original: at tu ne clam me comesses prandium . perge in virum .
sections.translation: But as for me , you shouldn ’ t have slily devoured the breakfast . ( To the WIFE .) Go on against your husband .

sections.label: Section 4.2.612
sections.translation: Won ’ t you hold your tongue ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.612b
sections.original: Non hercle vero taceo . nutat , ne loquar .
sections.translation: Faith , I really will not hold my tongue . ( To the WIFE .) He ’ s nodding to me not to speak .

sections.label: Section 4.2.613
sections.original: Non hercle ego quidem usquam quicquam nuto neque nicto tibi .
sections.translation: On my word , I really never did nod to you , or wink in any way .

sections.label: Section 4.2.614
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I certainly am a miserable woman .

sections.label: Section 4.2.614b
sections.original: Qui tu misera es ? mi expedi .
sections.translation: Why are you a miserable woman ? tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.615
sections.original: Nihil hoc confidentius , qui quae vides ea pernegat .
sections.translation: Nothing is more audacious than this man , who resolutely denies those things which you see .

sections.label: Section 4.2.616
sections.original: Per Iovem deosque omnis adiuro , uxor , ( satin hoc est tibi ?)
sections.translation: By Jupiter and all the Gods , I swear , wife , that I did not nod to him ; isn ’ t that enough for you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.617b
sections.original: Credit iam tibi de isto : illuc redi .
sections.translation: She now believes you about that matter ; go back again there .

sections.label: Section 4.2.618
sections.translation: Go back where ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.618b
sections.original: Equidem ad phrygionem censeo . ei , pallam refer .
sections.translation: Why , to the embroiderer , as I suppose . Go and bring the mantle back .

sections.label: Section 4.2.619
sections.translation: What mantle is it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.619b
sections.original: Taceo iam , quando haec rem non meminit suam .
sections.translation: Now I hold my tongue , since he doesn ’ t remember his own business .

sections.label: Section 4.2.620
sections.original: Numquis servorum deliquit ? num ancillae aut servi tibi
sections.translation: Has any one of the servants done amiss ? Do either the maid or the men-servants give you saucy answers ? Speak out ; it shan ’ t be done with impunity .

sections.label: Section 4.2.621b
sections.translation: You are trifling .

sections.label: Section 4.2.622
sections.original: Tristis admodum es . non mi istuc satis placet .
sections.translation: ⋯ she is very sorrowful ; this doesn ’ t quite please me , but I ’ ll speak to her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.623
sections.translation: Surely you are angry at some one of the domestics ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.623b
sections.translation: You are trifling .

sections.label: Section 4.2.624
sections.translation: Are you angry with me at all events ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.624b
sections.translation: Now you are not trifling .

sections.label: Section 4.2.625
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I haven ’ t done wrong in anything .

sections.label: Section 4.2.625b
sections.translation: Ah ! now you are trifling again .

sections.label: Section 4.2.626
sections.original: Dic , mea uxor , quid tibi aegre est ?
sections.translation: Tell me , my wife , what is it amiss with you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.626b
sections.translation: The pretty fellow ’ s soothing you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.627
sections.original: Potin ut mihi molestus ne sis ? num te appello ?
sections.translation: Can ’ t you cease being annoying to me ? Did I address you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.627b
sections.translation: Take yourself off —

sections.label: Section 4.2.628
sections.original: Sic datur . properato absente me comesse prandium ,
sections.translation: Such is the return . Do you make haste to eat up the breakfast in my absence ; and then in your drunkenness make fun of me , with your chaplet on , before the house .

sections.label: Section 4.2.630
sections.original: Neque edepol ego prandi neque hodie huc intro tetuli pedem .
sections.translation: By all the powers , I have neither breakfasted , nor have I this day set foot inside of that house .

sections.label: Section 4.2.631
sections.translation: Do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.631b
sections.translation: By my troth , I really do deny it .
